channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Carney’s government faces challenges maintaining majority amid potential MP departures
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney's Liberal government is navigating a precarious position with a very slim majority in the House of Commons. With MPs Jonathan Wilkinson and Nathaniel Erskine-Smith expected ...

item.title: Johannesburg father mourns four-year-old twins lost in shack fire
item.description: In Langlaagte, Johannesburg, four-year-old twins Isibabalo and Isiphiwe Xakwe tragically died in a shack fire at the Zamimpilo informal settlement. Their father, Abdul Ibrahim, recalls their last word...

item.title: Innisfil team of dogs set to compete for Canada in international flyball competition
item.description: A team of dogs from Innisfil is preparing to represent Canada at the Flyball Open World Cup, a prestigious international dog sport competition. Flyball is a fast-paced relay race where teams of four d...

item.title: President Ramaphosa Announces Judicial Appointments to Bolster Court Capacity
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has announced a fresh set of judicial appointments aimed at enhancing the efficiency and capacity of South Africa’s courts. These appointments, made in line with section 174(...

item.title: NiMet and CBN collaborate to improve national economic planning using weather and climate data
item.description: The Nigerian Meteorological Agency (NiMet) and the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) don waka come together, sign Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to dey share data to help improve economic forecasting a...

item.title: Remembering Giulietta Masina: Iconic Actress and Muse of Federico Fellini
item.description: Giulietta Masina was an extraordinary figure in world cinema, often hailed as the 'female Charlie Chaplin' for her unique ability to convey deep emotion through both comedy and tragedy. Born in Italy,...

item.title: Coca-Cola Supports Ruvu Basin Restoration to Improve Water Access for Dar es Salaam
item.description: Coca-Cola is chipping in USD 1.94 million to help restore the Ruvu Basin, one of the main sources of water for Dar es Salaam and surrounding areas. This project is very important because water demand ...

item.title: DHS and Canada to Test Autonomous Drones and Vehicles Over 5G Along Shared Border
item.description: The U.S. Department of Homeland Security (DHS), in collaboration with Defense Research and Development Canada (DRDC), is preparing an experiment this fall to test autonomous drones and ground vehicles...

item.title: Atlanta Man Sentenced for Theft of Beyoncé’s Hard Drives Containing Unreleased Material
item.description: Kelvin Evans has been sentenced to five years, including two years in custody, after pleading guilty to stealing hard drives containing unreleased Beyoncé music, tour plans, and other materials from a...

item.title: Behind the Scenes of Charles Chaplin’s Masterpiece 'City Lights' (1931)
item.description: Charles Chaplin’s 1931 film 'City Lights' stands as a testament to his extraordinary dedication and artistic vision. Serving as writer, director, producer, composer, and star, Chaplin crafted this sil...

item.title: PNP raises full alert following gunfire and lockdown at the Senate
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) declared full alert status on May 13, 2026, after gunshots were reported at the Senate, coinciding with a lockdown at the upper chamber where Senator Ronald Dela R...

item.title: London Police Prepare Major Operation for Rival Rallies During FA Cup Final
item.description: London is set to witness an exceptionally large police operation coinciding with the day of the FA Cup final, as authorities prepare to manage rival public demonstrations. The operation, described as ...

item.title: State Department Insider Raises Concerns Over Unreviewed Presidential Directives and Potential Election Interference
item.description: A State Department insider has raised concerns that President Donald Trump may be preparing to use Presidential Emergency Action Documents (PEADs), which have never been reviewed by Congress or the co...

item.title: Overview of SABIS International School in Runda: Curriculum, Fees, and Facilities
item.description: SABIS International School in Runda, Nairobi, opened its doors in 2018 and is now among Kenya’s top international learning institutions. The school is owned by Centum Investments in collaboration with...

item.title: Dodoma Jiji beat Yanga, keeping Simba in title race
item.description: In an unexpected turn of events in Tanzanian Premier League football, Dodoma Jiji pulled off a surprising win against Yanga, shaking the league standings and giving Simba a renewed chance at the champ...

item.title: Supporters of Senator Bato gather outside Senate following reduced security after shooting incident
item.description: Supporters of Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa have set up a camp outside the Senate premises after authorities relaxed security measures following a recent shooting incident. The incident, which cause...

item.title: Home Affairs Ministry Sets Up Road Safety Committees Across Tanzania
item.description: Dar es Salaam — The Ministry of Home Affairs, through the National Road Safety Council, has officially appointed chairpersons, members, and secretaries for Road Safety Committees in all 31 police regi...

item.title: Lions Club Plans Empowerment Programmes for Vulnerable Communities in Niger Delta
item.description: Members of the Lions Clubs International don hold their 19th Multiple District Convention for Asaba, Delta State. The theme for the event na 'Building Bridges of Service and Unity'. People from differ...

item.title: Abuja Court Martial Postpones Coup Trial and Mandates Dress Code for Lawyers
item.description: Di Abuja court martial don adjourn di trial wey dey involve some alleged coup plotters go May 18. Di tribunal don order say all di lawyers wey dey represent di accused make dem show for court in wigs ...

item.title: Heat Index Expected to Reach Dangerous Levels in Nearly 50 Areas on Thursday
item.description: The Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA) has issued warnings that almost 50 areas across the country are expected to experience dangerous heat index le...
